Trend Radar
Beast Industries
June 2 – June 8, 2025
Weekly Trending Report
Curated signals across IG, TikTok, and YouTube — with ideas for how we beastify them. Updated weekly.
Patterns & learnings this week
1
Repeatability beats novelty for virality
Europeans trying American food and the Guess the Weight format both win because zero equipment or prior knowledge is required. The concept anyone can do travels furthest. Our power tools overperformer works for the same reason — the format is the hook, not the prop.
2
World Cup is a rare window where global + local content converges
Europeans reacting to American food is culturally universal, needs no backstory, and is peaking now. Strong 4–6 week window for a European creator collab (KSI?) or a Brazilian creator play. Arthur Lima (1.7b views in March) is the most obvious partner target right now.
3
6 of 10 top trending TT creators are in the pet + AI niche — low applicability, worth flagging
AI-generated media is clearly driving massive reach right now. Not our lane, but it signals that algorithmically optimised, non-human content is competing for attention. Friend group challenge creators just outside the top 10 (Moon Squad, The Smily Fam) are more directly useful for format ideation.
4
Red Bull is a brand to benchmark — simple visual game mechanics with strong first frames
Skydiving tic tac toe (9m) is the clearest example this week of a concept that's instantly readable from a single frame. Their format design standard — one visual premise, one POV, no explanation needed — is exactly what we should be targeting for Shorts.

Trending sounds
🎵
"Cinderella" — hype your partner or best friend
Week of June 2–8 · #1 trending TT sound
TT montage formatemotionalduos
People using it to hype up their partner or best friend. Montage format — short clips cut to the track. If we had Trial Reels on TT, a cute Jimmy + Thea montage would be a natural fit. For the animated show: is there a best friend duo we could edit to this? cc Jake Isler.

Idea inspo & brand references
🎯
Red Bull — skydiving tic tac toe
9m views · strong visual first frame
IG brand referencestrong first framesimple game mechanic
9m views on a concept that's instantly readable from a single frame — a giant tic tac toe grid from a skydiving POV. This is the visual game format standard we should be benchmarking. Their soccer wall accuracy challenge (7m) follows the same formula.
Skydiving tic tac toe Soccer wall challenge

Beast Industries · format benchmark

Referenced as the collab hook for the Europeans/American food format — KSI + power tools + World Cup cultural moment. Also a useful internal benchmark for what our short-form format baseline looks like when it works.
